Envelopes

Envelopes describe a contour using a series of frames. More...

Functions

CSynToken CSyn_CreateEnvelope (CSynContext context, uint32 numFrames)
 Create an envelope resource with the given number of frames and channels.

CSynErr CSyn_WriteEnvelope (CSynContext context, CSynToken env, uint32 startFrame, EnvelopeFrame frames[], uint32 numFrames)
 Write an array of envelope frames from the application memory space into the internal JSyn Sample memory.

CSynErr CSyn_ReadEnvelope (CSynContext context, CSynToken env, uint32 startFrame, EnvelopeFrame frames[], uint32 numFrames)
 Read an array of envelope frames from the internal JSyn Sample memory into the application memory space.

Detailed Description

Envelopes describe a contour using a series of frames.

Each frames consists of a duration, and a value. The duration is the length of time it takes to go from the previous value to the new frames value. Envelopes can be played using the unit CSYN_UNIT_Envelope_Linear. Envelope data can only be played sequentially from one frame to the next.

Function Documentation

CSynToken CSyn_CreateEnvelope (  CSynContext  context,
uint32  numFrames
) 
 

Create an envelope resource with the given number of frames and channels.

The value of numFrames must be positive and is limited by the amount of available memory.

CSynErr CSyn_ReadEnvelope (  CSynContext  context,
CSynToken  env,
uint32  startFrame,
EnvelopeFrame  frames[],
uint32  numFrames
) 
 

Read an array of envelope frames from the internal JSyn Sample memory into the application memory space.

The parameter startFrame is the first frame in the Envelope that will be read.

CSynErr CSyn_WriteEnvelope (  CSynContext  context,
CSynToken  env,
uint32  startFrame,
EnvelopeFrame  frames[],
uint32  numFrames
) 
 

Write an array of envelope frames from the application memory space into the internal JSyn Sample memory.

The parameter startFrame is the first frame in the Envelope that will be written.
